title: Derivative Tower Collapses at Order 1 — resp_z Family Drives Only One Stage

content.finding: The resp_z derivative tower collapses at order 1. resp_z has autocorrelation 0.98, delta_rz has 0.11, delta_delta_rz has -0.48 (anti-correlated). The resp_z signal family can drive exactly ONE stage of adaptation (Stage 2, alpha).
content.evidence: Lag-1 autocorrelation measured across 3 seeds, all 72 cells. Order 0: mean=+0.9823, Order 1: mean=+0.1090 (range -0.04 to +0.83, high variance), Order 2: mean=-0.4838 (anti-correlated, oscillation).
implications: Stage 3 failure EXPLAINED: delta_rz lacks temporal structure (0.11) to drive reliable eta adaptation
The resp_z signal family is EXHAUSTED after Stage 2 — no higher-order derivatives are usable
Stages 3+ require a FUNDAMENTALLY DIFFERENT self-generated signal, not derivatives of resp_z
The Living Seed substrate is not necessarily at its ceiling — but the resp_z mechanism is
Need to search for alternative intrinsic signals: cumulative/windowed averages, frequency-domain, inter-cell correlations, or structural signals
content.key_insight: The derivative tower (resp_z → delta_rz → delta_delta_rz) is a quantitative measure of how many stages of adaptation a signal family can drive. For resp_z, the answer is exactly 1. This is a fundamental property of the system dynamics, not a mechanism failure.
alternative_signals_to_explore: Windowed/cumulative delta_rz (integrate over N steps to recover structure)
Frequency-domain analysis of resp_z (spectral vs temporal autocorrelation)
Inter-cell correlation patterns (spatial rather than temporal derivatives)
Information geometry of the alpha distribution itself

constraints_implied: resp_z derivative tower collapses at order 1 — higher-order derivatives cannot drive adaptation
Stage 3+ requires a fundamentally different signal family, not derivatives of resp_z
